framework.name: NIST Cybersecurity Framework
framework.description: Official NIST Cybersecurity Framework 2.0 catalog. During corpus load, active subcategory controls are generated from the local export file when it is present.

controls.title: Organizational Context
controls.description: The circumstances, stakeholder expectations, dependencies, and legal obligations surrounding cybersecurity risk decisions are understood.
controls.guidance: Document the mission context, dependency map, and external obligations that shape cybersecurity decisions.

controls.title: Risk Management Strategy
controls.description: The organization establishes priorities, constraints, and risk appetite statements to support operational risk decisions.
controls.guidance: Set measurable risk objectives and communicate how risk will be evaluated and prioritized.

controls.title: Roles, Responsibilities, and Authorities
controls.description: Cybersecurity responsibilities and authorities are defined to drive accountability, assessment, and improvement.
controls.guidance: Assign owners for risk decisions, oversight, and execution across the organization.

controls.title: Policy
controls.description: Organizational cybersecurity policy is established, communicated, and enforced.
controls.guidance: Maintain an approved cybersecurity policy set and refresh it when risk conditions change.

controls.title: Oversight
controls.description: Risk management results and performance are reviewed to adjust the cybersecurity strategy.
controls.guidance: Use KPIs, KRIs, and review cycles to steer strategy updates and leadership actions.

controls.title: Cybersecurity Supply Chain Risk Management
controls.description: Cybersecurity supply chain risk processes are established, managed, monitored, and improved by stakeholders.
controls.guidance: Integrate supplier risk requirements into onboarding, contracts, monitoring, and termination.

controls.title: Asset Management
controls.description: Assets that enable business purposes are identified and managed according to their importance and risk.
controls.guidance: Keep current inventories for systems, software, services, data, and supporting dependencies.

controls.title: Risk Assessment
controls.description: Cybersecurity risk to the organization, its assets, and affected individuals is understood.
controls.guidance: Continuously assess threats, vulnerabilities, likelihoods, impacts, and planned responses.

controls.title: Improvement
controls.description: Improvements to cybersecurity processes, procedures, and activities are identified across the CSF functions.
controls.guidance: Feed findings from reviews, testing, and operations into measurable program improvement work.

controls.title: Identity Management, Authentication, and Access Control
controls.description: Access to physical and logical assets is limited to authorized users, services, and hardware based on risk.
controls.guidance: Manage identities, strengthen authentication, and review entitlements against least privilege.

controls.title: Awareness and Training
controls.description: Personnel receive the awareness and training needed to perform their cybersecurity-related tasks.
controls.guidance: Deliver recurring general and role-specific training tied to the risks people can influence.

controls.title: Data Security
controls.description: Data is managed according to the organization risk strategy to protect confidentiality, integrity, and availability.
controls.guidance: Protect data at rest, in transit, and in use with classification, encryption, and backup controls.

controls.title: Platform Security
controls.description: Hardware, software, and services on physical and virtual platforms are managed to protect their security properties.
controls.guidance: Secure platforms with hardened baselines, patching, logging, and software execution controls.

controls.title: Technology Infrastructure Resilience
controls.description: Security architectures are managed to protect assets and maintain resilience under normal and adverse conditions.
controls.guidance: Segment environments and design infrastructure to tolerate disruption and recover gracefully.

controls.title: Continuous Monitoring
controls.description: Assets are monitored to find anomalies, indicators of compromise, and other potentially adverse events.
controls.guidance: Monitor networks, endpoints, services, and physical environments for abnormal behavior.

controls.title: Adverse Event Analysis
controls.description: Potentially adverse events are analyzed to characterize the activity and determine whether incidents exist.
controls.guidance: Correlate multiple data sources so analysts can triage and declare incidents consistently.

controls.title: Incident Management
controls.description: Responses to detected cybersecurity incidents are managed through triage, coordination, and execution.
controls.guidance: Run incidents through a defined process for validation, prioritization, escalation, and action.

controls.title: Incident Analysis
controls.description: Investigations are conducted to support effective response, forensics, and recovery planning.
controls.guidance: Capture evidence, reconstruct events, and establish impact and root cause for each incident.

controls.title: Incident Response Reporting and Communication
controls.description: Response activities are coordinated with internal and external stakeholders as required by policy or law.
controls.guidance: Define who must be notified, what must be shared, and how updates are coordinated.

controls.title: Incident Mitigation
controls.description: Activities are performed to contain incidents, limit expansion, and reduce harmful effects.
controls.guidance: Provide responders with approved containment and eradication actions for common incident classes.

controls.title: Incident Recovery Plan Execution
controls.description: Restoration activities are performed to return systems and services affected by incidents to operational use.
controls.guidance: Recover in a defined order, verify restoration integrity, and confirm operational normalcy.

controls.title: Incident Recovery Communication
controls.description: Recovery activities are coordinated and communicated with internal and external parties.
controls.guidance: Coordinate recovery messaging so leaders, partners, and affected parties receive accurate updates.
